template: Применение предложенной методики позволило увеличить ___ на ___
when_to_use: Comparison with baseline

template: Полученные значения ___ составляют ___
when_to_use: Results presentation

template: В качестве базового варианта выбран ___ по причине ___
when_to_use: Experimental design section

template: Преимуществом метода ___ по сравнению с ___ является ___
when_to_use: Comparative analysis in survey

template: В литературе недостаточно освещен вопрос ___
when_to_use: Transition to research motivation
common_mistakes: Gap already addressed by recent work

template: Основным ограничением существующих подходов является ___
when_to_use: Gap identification section

function: Position own work relative to prior art
template: Настоящая работа развивает подход ___ с учетом ___
when_to_use: After literature review

template: Существующие методы проектирования конструкций из КМ можно разделить на ___
when_to_use: Literature review chapter

function: 总结本研究的主要贡献
template: Основными результатами работы являются: 1) ___, 2) ___.
when_to_use: 结论章节开头，概括核心贡献
common_mistakes: 贡献罗列与摘要或引言重复

template: Дальнейшие исследования могут быть направлены на ___.
when_to_use: 结论章节结尾，展望后续工作
common_mistakes: 方向过于宽泛，与本文关联度弱

function: 解释观察到的现象背后的物理或化学机制
template: Наблюдаемый эффект может быть объяснен ___.
when_to_use: 讨论章节，对实验结果进行深入解释
common_mistakes: 解释缺乏理论或文献支持

function: 讨论不同因素之间的权衡关系
template: Повышение ___ приводит к снижению ___.
when_to_use: 讨论部分，分析设计或工艺参数间的制约关系
common_mistakes: 未能明确指出权衡的具体表现

function: 评估技术方案在实际应用中的复杂性
template: Практическая реализация ___ связана с трудностями ___.
when_to_use: 讨论技术应用或结论中，评估可行性
common_mistakes: 复杂性描述过于主观，缺乏具体因素

function: 定义用于评估实验结果的指标
template: Качество оценивалось по следующим метрикам: ___.
when_to_use: 实验方法或结果分析章节
common_mistakes: 指标选择缺乏合理性解释

function: 强调实验的可重复性条件
template: Каждый эксперимент повторялся ___ раз для обеспечения ___.
when_to_use: 实验设计部分，说明数据可靠性
common_mistakes: 未说明重复次数或统计方法

function: 描述实验所模拟的工况或场景
template: Эксперименты проводились в режиме ___ при значениях ___.
when_to_use: 实验设计部分，定义实验条件
common_mistakes: 工况描述不完整，缺少关键参数

function: 指出研究领域的技术挑战与需求
template: Обработка ___ требует ___ в условиях ___.
when_to_use: 论文引言开头，阐述研究背景和动机
common_mistakes: 动机过于宽泛，未与具体工艺关联

function: 清晰陈述研究的主要目标
template: Целью работы является исследование ___ для ___.
when_to_use: 引言结尾或单独段落，直接点明研究目标
common_mistakes: 目标描述为过程而非可验证的结果

function: 明确指出当前研究的具体问题
template: В настоящее время недостаточно изучены особенности ___ в процессе ___.
when_to_use: 引言部分，陈述研究要解决的具体知识空白
common_mistakes: 问题陈述过于笼统，未聚焦于特定物理或工艺环节

function: 说明具体实施步骤或设备配置细节
template: Для измерения ___ использовалась установка, состоящая из ___.
when_to_use: 实验设置或方法细节部分
common_mistakes: 细节描述缺乏关键参数或连接逻辑

function: 概述方法或实验的整体流程
template: Исследование включает следующие этапы: 1) ___, 2) ___.
when_to_use: 方法论章节开头，提供研究的整体框架
common_mistakes: 流程描述过于细节化，失去整体观

function: 陈述理论模型或分析所基于的假设
template: В модели предполагается, что ___ является ___.
when_to_use: 理论建模章节开头或方法论说明部分
common_mistakes: 假设过于理想化且未讨论其合理性

function: 定义模型的适用范围或边界条件
template: Расчет выполнен для условий, когда ___ находится в диапазоне ___.
when_to_use: 理论模型部分，说明模型的有效边界
common_mistakes: 未明确界定参数的有效范围

function: 说明模型或系统受到的限制
template: Основными ограничениями модели являются ___ и ___.
when_to_use: 模型讨论部分，诚实地说明其局限
common_mistakes: 忽略讨论模型的约束条件

function: 通过表格呈现不同条件下的结果对比
template: В таблице ___ представлено сравнение ___ для различных ___.
when_to_use: 结果部分，系统展示数据对比
common_mistakes: 表格数据与正文叙述重复且缺乏解读

template: Значение ___ составило ___ при ___.
when_to_use: 实验结果章节，陈述关键发现
common_mistakes: 数值缺少单位或测量条件

function: 明确指出研究领域的空白
template: В литературе отсутствуют данные по ___ для ___.
when_to_use: 紧接在局限性陈述之后，引出本研究的必要性
common_mistakes: 空白描述过于宽泛，无法指导具体研究

function: 指出前人研究的不足或局限性
template: Существующие подходы имеют ограничения в части ___.
when_to_use: 综述部分，为本研究定位提供依据
common_mistakes: 批评过于笼统，未指出具体技术瓶颈

function: 对已有技术或方法进行分类
template: Методы исследования ___ можно классифицировать по ___.
when_to_use: 文献综述章节，梳理现有方法体系
common_mistakes: 分类标准不明确或相互重叠

function: 连接段落或章节间的逻辑关系
template: В соответствии с задачами исследования, ___ позволяет ___.
when_to_use: 章节或段落之间，承上启下
common_mistakes: 逻辑连接生硬，缺乏过渡

function: 开启新章节或新主题的引导句
template: В данной главе исследуется ___.
when_to_use: 每一章的起始段落
common_mistakes: 章节引言过于冗长，未快速切入主题

function: Interpreting why the proposed method works well
template: The superior performance is likely due to the ___ component, which effectively captures ___.
when_to_use: To move from 'what' happened to 'why' it happened.
common_mistakes: Making causal claims that are not supported by the evidence.
Attributing success to the wrong component.

function: Discussing how sensitive the results are to certain parameters
template: The method's performance is robust to variations in ___, but sensitive to ___.
when_to_use: To understand the operational boundaries and reliability.
common_mistakes: Not providing evidence (e.g., a plot) for the sensitivity claim.

function: Identifying the baseline methods for comparison
template: We compare our approach against several state-of-the-art methods, including ___.
when_to_use: To establish the comparative context for the experiments.
common_mistakes: Choosing weak baselines that make the proposed method look good.
Not including a relevant, recent baseline.

function: Describing the datasets used for evaluation
template: We evaluate our method on ___ benchmark datasets, including ___.
when_to_use: At the start of the experimental section.
common_mistakes: Not providing enough detail for reproducibility.

template: Performance is evaluated using ___ metrics, including ___.
when_to_use: Before presenting results, to define how success is measured.
common_mistakes: Using metrics that don't align with the problem's objective.
Reporting only one metric when multiple aspects are important.

function: Previewing the main novel contributions of the work
template: The key contributions of this dissertation are: ___.
when_to_use: Often at the end of the introduction to summarize what is new.
common_mistakes: Claiming contributions that are incremental or not clearly novel.
Using overly technical language for a preview.

function: Establishing the research context and significance
template: Recent advances in ___ have led to increased interest in ___ for ___.
when_to_use: At the start of the introduction to frame the broader context of the work.
common_mistakes: Using overly generic terms that don't specify the research niche.
Failing to connect the broad trend to the specific problem.

function: Stating the high-level goal of the research
template: The primary objective of this research is to develop a framework/method for ___.
when_to_use: Following the problem statement to declare the overarching aim.
common_mistakes: Confusing objectives with methods or tasks.
Using passive voice that obscures agency.

function: Clearly defining the core research problem
template: This work addresses the problem of ___ under ___.
when_to_use: After establishing motivation, to precisely state what the thesis solves.
common_mistakes: Being too vague or too narrow.

function: Listing the concrete research tasks undertaken
template: To achieve this objective, the following specific tasks are addressed: ___.
when_to_use: When outlining the research steps in the introduction.
common_mistakes: Listing tasks that don't logically follow from the objective.

function: Outlining the organization of the document
template: The remainder of this thesis is organized as follows: ___.
when_to_use: At the very end of the introduction to guide the reader.
common_mistakes: Providing a list without any conceptual grouping.
Forgetting to mention appendices or supplementary material.

function: Describing the sequential steps of an algorithm
template: The core learning algorithm iteratively performs the following: 1) ___, 2) ___, 3) ___.
when_to_use: When detailing an algorithmic procedure.
common_mistakes: Mixing high-level concepts with low-level implementation details.

function: Analyzing the computational complexity of the method
template: The time complexity of the proposed algorithm is O(___), with space complexity of O(___).
when_to_use: To assess the scalability and feasibility of the method.
common_mistakes: Providing asymptotic complexity without explaining the dominant terms.
Ignoring the complexity of preprocessing steps.

function: Justifying the choice of hyperparameters or settings
template: The parameter ___ is set to ___ based on ___.
when_to_use: When discussing experimental setup or implementation choices.
common_mistakes: Stating a value without any justification.
Not mentioning if the value was tuned.

function: Providing a high-level description of the method pipeline
template: The proposed method consists of three main stages: ___, ___, and ___.
when_to_use: At the beginning of a method chapter to give an overview.
common_mistakes: Providing too many stages that overwhelm the reader.
Not explaining how the stages connect.

function: Stating the foundational assumptions of the proposed model
template: The following assumptions are made regarding the system/process: ___.
when_to_use: At the beginning of a model formulation chapter to set boundaries.
common_mistakes: Listing assumptions that are not later validated or discussed.
Hiding critical assumptions in the text rather than listing them.

function: Specifying the constraints governing the model or problem
template: The solution must satisfy the following constraints: ___.
when_to_use: When defining an optimization problem or a formal framework.
common_mistakes: Presenting constraints that are mathematically inconsistent.
Not explaining the physical or practical origin of constraints.

template: Let ___ denote the ___. The set of all ___ is denoted by ___.
when_to_use: In a notation section or at the first use of a key symbol.
common_mistakes: Using inconsistent notation throughout the thesis.
Not defining symbols where they are first used.

function: Providing qualitative examples to illustrate the method's behavior
template: Figure X shows a representative case where our method ___, while the baseline ___.
when_to_use: To provide intuitive understanding beyond aggregate numbers.
common_mistakes: Failing to explain what the figure demonstrates.

function: Emphasizing the gain of the proposed method over others
template: Compared to ___, the proposed method yields a relative improvement of ___% in ___.
when_to_use: To highlight the advantage of the contribution.
common_mistakes: Using relative improvement on metrics where it's misleading.

function: Presenting quantitative results in a standardized way
template: On the ___ dataset, our method achieves an average ___ of ___, outperforming the best baseline by ___%.
when_to_use: When reporting the main experimental outcome.
common_mistakes: Reporting only averages without variance (e.g., standard deviation).
Not specifying which run (e.g., mean over 5 runs) is reported.

function: Comparing the strengths and weaknesses of existing methods
template: Method A excels in ___ but struggles with ___, while Method B offers ___ at the cost of ___.
when_to_use: When analyzing the trade-offs in the literature.
common_mistakes: Not citing specific papers for each method.

function: Articulating the research gap the thesis aims to fill
template: To date, no method has successfully integrated ___ with ___.
when_to_use: Directly after discussing limitations to state the void.
common_mistakes: Claiming a gap that is too broad or already addressed.
Not connecting the gap directly to the thesis objective.

function: Identifying shortcomings in the existing research
template: A significant limitation of prior work is the assumption that ___.
when_to_use: To motivate the need for the current research.
common_mistakes: Stating limitations without providing evidence or citations.

function: Situating the current work relative to the literature
template: This work builds upon ___ by introducing ___, which addresses ___.
when_to_use: Often in the concluding part of the related work chapter.
common_mistakes: Overclaiming novelty relative to the cited work.
Failing to acknowledge relevant prior art.

function: Classifying prior work into a structured framework
template: Existing approaches can be broadly categorized based on their ___.
when_to_use: In the literature review to provide an organized overview.
common_mistakes: Creating a taxonomy that is not mutually exclusive or collectively exhaustive.
Using categories that are not relevant to the thesis problem.

template: Основные научные результаты, выносимые на защиту: ___ ; ___ ; ___ .
when_to_use: Introduction section listing defended contributions
common_mistakes: Contributions not specific or not verifiable

function: State practical motivation for the research topic
template: Микрофлюидные сенсоры потока находят широкое применение в ___ , что обуславливает актуальность их разработки.
when_to_use: Opening paragraph of Introduction to justify research relevance
common_mistakes: Too vague without specifying application domain

template: Целью настоящей работы является разработка микрофлюидного теплового сенсора потока с использованием технологии ___ для обеспечения ___ .
when_to_use: Objective formulation in Introduction
common_mistakes: Objective too broad or not measurable

template: Несмотря на достигнутые результаты, существующие сенсоры потока обладают ограничениями по ___ , что не позволяет использовать их для ___ .
when_to_use: Problem statement section of Introduction
common_mistakes: Listing limitations without linking to unsolved tasks

template: Разработка ___ соответствует приоритетным направлениям развития науки и технологий в области ___ .
when_to_use: Relevance paragraph of Introduction
common_mistakes: Claiming relevance without citing strategic documents

template: Для достижения поставленной цели решены следующие задачи: ___ ; ___ ; ___ ; ___ .
when_to_use: Task enumeration in Introduction
common_mistakes: Tasks not directly mapping to chapters

template: Диссертация состоит из введения, ___ глав, заключения и списка литературы. В первой главе ___ , во второй — ___ , в третьей — ___ , в четвёртой — ___ .
when_to_use: End of Introduction describing thesis layout
common_mistakes: Describing chapters without connecting to tasks

function: Describe ablation or parameter variation plan
template: Для оценки влияния ___ на характеристики сенсора проведены эксперименты с варьированием ___ в диапазоне от ___ до ___ .
when_to_use: Design of experiments or sensitivity analysis section

template: Решение задачи проводится в следующей последовательности: (1) ___ ; (2) ___ ; (3) ___ .
when_to_use: Algorithm description section

template: Критическим параметром процесса ___ является ___ , которое контролируется методом ___ .
when_to_use: Detailing sensitive fabrication steps

function: Define input and output of a processing step
template: На входе этапа ___ имеется ___ , на выходе получается ___ .
when_to_use: Describing each process step in fabrication chapter

template: Параметры процесса ___ : температура — ___ °C, давление — ___ , время — ___ .
when_to_use: Describing fabrication or experimental parameters

function: Provide overview of the fabrication process
template: Маршрутный технологический процесс изготовления микрофлюидного сенсора потока включает следующие этапы: ___ → ___ → ___ → ___ → ___ .
when_to_use: Opening of methodology chapter describing process flow

template: При численном моделировании приняты следующие допущения: ___ ; ___ ; ___ .
when_to_use: Model formulation section describing assumptions

template: Граничные условия на входе задаются как ___ , на выходе — как ___ , на стенках канала — как ___ .
when_to_use: Simulation setup section

template: Модель применима в диапазоне ___ ≤ ___ ≤ ___ , что ограничено ___ .
when_to_use: Defining validity range of computational model

template: Обозначим через ___ — ___ , через ___ — ___ .
when_to_use: First occurrence of mathematical notation in model chapter

template: Из экспериментальных данных однозначно определяются параметры ___ , тогда как ___ требует дополнительных измерений.
when_to_use: Discussion of model calibration feasibility

template: Упрощение модели путём пренебрежения ___ допустимо, поскольку при ___ вклад данной компоненты составляет менее ___ .
when_to_use: Justifying reduced-order model

template: Основными источниками неопределённости модели являются: ___ и ___ , которые оцениваются как ___ .
when_to_use: Model validation section discussing uncertainty

template: В качестве базовых методов для сравнения выбраны ___ , поскольку они представляют основные классы ___ и описаны в работах ___ .
when_to_use: Selecting baselines for experimental comparison

function: Compare existing approaches on selected criteria
template: Сравнение существующих подходов по критериям ___ показывает, что тепловые сенсоры обеспечивают ___ по сравнению с ___ .
when_to_use: Comparison table discussion in literature review

template: Критериями оценки эффективности микрофлюидного сенсора потока являются: ___ , ___ , ___ и ___ .
when_to_use: Defining evaluation framework in literature review
common_mistakes: Criteria not measurable or too subjective

template: Таким образом, в литературе отсутствуют работы, посвящённые ___ с одновременным обеспечением ___ .
when_to_use: End of literature review before transition to own work

template: Основными ограничениями существующих микрофлюидных сенсоров потока являются: ___ , ___ и ___ .
when_to_use: Gap identification section of literature review
common_mistakes: Listing too many limitations diluting focus

function: Position own work relative to prior art
template: В отличие от работ ___ , в настоящей работе предложен подход, основанный на ___ , что позволяет ___ .
when_to_use: Transition from survey to own methodology

template: Сенсоры потока жидкости можно классифицировать по принципу действия на ___ , ___ и ___ .
when_to_use: Literature review opening or section introducing sensor classification

function: Lists the main contributions of the research
template: The main contributions of this work include ___.
when_to_use: In the final conclusion to explicitly state what the research has achieved.

function: Summarizes the work completed in a chapter
template: The results presented in this chapter demonstrate ___.
slots: a viable method for grating recording
the validity of the coupled-wave model
when_to_use: At the end of a chapter (e.g., 'Conclusions for the first chapter') to recap key findings.
common_mistakes: Simply listing tasks performed instead of stating achievements.

function: Proposes a physical mechanism to explain an observed result
template: The observed ___ can be attributed to ___, which becomes dominant when ___.
slots: experimental phenomenon (e.g., a shoulder in the selectivity curve)
a proposed cause (e.g., interplay between grating periods)
a condition (e.g., high spatial frequency multiplexing)
when_to_use: In the discussion section, to interpret non-trivial results.
common_mistakes: Stating a correlation as a proven causal mechanism.

function: Proposes a physical or technical mechanism for an observation
template: This effect can be explained by the mechanism of ___.
when_to_use: In the discussion to provide a deeper understanding of a result.
common_mistakes: Proposing a mechanism without supporting evidence from the data.

function: Discusses the sensitivity of results to a parameter
template: The performance of the system is sensitive to ___.
slots: the spectral composition of the source
when_to_use: When analyzing how changes in an input affect the outcome in the discussion.
common_mistakes: Stating sensitivity without indicating the direction of effect.

function: Describes practical steps in an experimental process
template: The implementation involved ___ for ___.
slots: achieving the required refractive index change
when_to_use: When detailing the practical execution of an experimental method.
common_mistakes: Being overly vague about the process.

function: Describes the sample or dataset used in an experiment
template: The experiments were conducted on ___ samples of ___, with a thickness of ___ and a size of ___.
when_to_use: In the 'Experimental Conditions' section.
common_mistakes: Omitting key specifications that affect reproducibility.

function: Defines a quantitative measure used to evaluate performance
template: The diffraction efficiency η is defined as the ratio of ___ to ___.
when_to_use: When introducing how results will be quantified.
common_mistakes: Using a non-standard metric without clear definition.

function: Mentions steps taken to ensure experimental reproducibility
template: To ensure reproducibility, ___ was controlled within ___, and ___ was calibrated using ___.
slots: a critical variable (e.g., temperature, laser alignment)
when_to_use: In the methods section, after describing the procedure.
common_mistakes: Being overly generic (e.g., 'standard procedures were followed').

function: Describes the design of a test scenario or case study
template: A test scenario was designed to investigate the effect of ___.
when_to_use: When detailing how experiments were structured to test a specific hypothesis.
common_mistakes: Designing scenarios that do not isolate the variable of interest.

template: Experiments were conducted under ___ conditions, including ___.
when_to_use: At the beginning of the experimental section to set the context.

function: Introduces a formal definition within the theoretical model
template: Let ___ be defined as ___.
when_to_use: When formally defining a key mathematical concept or object in the model chapter.
common_mistakes: Using informal language for a formal definition.

template: The key contributions include: the formulation of ___, the experimental demonstration of ___, and the analysis of ___.
slots: a new theoretical model or approach
the influence of a parameter on performance
when_to_use: At the end of the introduction chapter to outline expected contributions.
common_mistakes: Overpromising contributions not fully substantiated in the thesis.

function: States the main goal of the thesis clearly
template: The objective of this work is to develop and experimentally validate ___ for ___.
slots: a specific application (e.g., angular multiplexing in PTR glass)
when_to_use: Directly after the problem statement.
common_mistakes: Combining multiple disparate objectives into one vague sentence.

function: States the primary goal of the research
template: The primary objective of this study is to ___.
when_to_use: Near the beginning of the introduction to state the research aim clearly.
common_mistakes: Being too vague or overly broad.

function: Identifies a specific gap or challenge in the current technology
template: However, existing ___ exhibit limitations in ___, such as ___.
when_to_use: To pinpoint the technical problem the thesis aims to solve.
common_mistakes: Making the problem statement too vague.

function: Establishes the practical importance of the research topic
template: The development of ___ requires the implementation of ___ to achieve ___.
slots: the target technology (e.g., compact AR displays)
when_to_use: At the beginning of the introduction to motivate the study.
common_mistakes: Stating relevance without connecting to specific challenges.

function: Breaks down the main objective into concrete research tasks
template: This involves the following tasks: ___, ___, and ___.
when_to_use: Immediately following the objective statement.
common_mistakes: Listing tasks that are not logically sequenced.

function: Describes a step in a computational or analytical algorithm
template: First, ___ is calculated using ___. Then, ___ is determined from ___.
when_to_use: When explaining a numerical simulation or data analysis procedure.
common_mistakes: Using vague sequential words like 'next' without clear dependency.

function: Specifies the value or range of a critical experimental parameter
template: The recording was performed with a ___ of ___ (±___).
slots: parameter name (e.g., exposure energy, spatial frequency)
when_to_use: When detailing the conditions of a specific experiment.
common_mistakes: Failing to state units or uncertainty.

function: Describes how a specific parameter value was chosen
template: The parameter ___ was set to ___ to ensure ___.
when_to_use: In the methods section to justify critical experimental settings.

function: Provides a high-level overview of the experimental or numerical workflow
template: The experimental procedure consists of ___, followed by ___, and concludes with ___.
when_to_use: At the beginning of the experimental chapter.
common_mistakes: Omitting crucial intermediate steps in the pipeline.

function: Describes a core process or method in the work
template: The main approach for ___ involves ___.
when_to_use: When introducing a key experimental or computational procedure.
common_mistakes: Providing too much detail in the overview sentence.

function: Lists the key assumptions underlying the mathematical model
template: The model assumes ___ and neglects the influence of ___.
slots: a primary condition (e.g., idealized grating planes)
secondary effects (e.g., material scattering, thermal noise)
when_to_use: At the start of the modeling chapter to define the scope.
common_mistakes: Not justifying why certain effects can be neglected.

function: States a simplifying assumption made in the model
template: The model assumes that ___.
when_to_use: At the beginning of a theoretical derivation to set boundaries.
common_mistakes: Forgetting to list key assumptions, leading to misuse of the model.

function: Introduces a key symbol and its meaning for the first time
template: We denote ___ by the symbol ___.
slots: a physical quantity (e.g., the refractive index modulation amplitude)
when_to_use: When defining the variables and symbols for the mathematical model.
common_mistakes: Overloading a symbol with multiple meanings.

template: Let ___ denote ___.
when_to_use: When first introducing a mathematical symbol in the model section.
common_mistakes: Using the same symbol for different quantities.

function: Describes the calculation or determination of model parameters
template: The parameters ___ are calculated based on ___.
when_to_use: When explaining how specific model parameters are derived from theory or experiment.

function: Describes a simplification made to make the problem tractable
template: For analytical tractability, the problem is simplified by assuming ___.
slots: a specific simplification (e.g., paraxial approximation, uniform grating modulation)
when_to_use: When presenting the core theoretical framework.
common_mistakes: Using a simplification that critically distorts the physical reality.

function: Presents a detailed example from the experimental results
template: For the sample fabricated under condition ___, the resulting ___ showed ___, as illustrated in Fig. ___.
slots: a key observation or peak value
when_to_use: To discuss individual results that support the main conclusions.
common_mistakes: Choosing a case that is not representative of the overall findings.

function: Presents a specific example or case from the results
template: For the case of ___, the results showed ___.
when_to_use: To illustrate a general trend with a concrete example from the data.

function: Quantifies the improvement of a new method over a baseline
template: The proposed method achieves an efficiency of ___, representing a ___% improvement over the conventional approach.
when_to_use: To highlight the advantage of the developed technique.
common_mistakes: Comparing absolute values without normalizing for fair comparison.

function: Reports a key numerical result with units and uncertainty
template: The measured diffraction efficiency was ___% ± ___%, for a grating recorded with ___.
slots: the corresponding condition (e.g., an exposure dose)
when_to_use: When presenting quantitative findings.
common_mistakes: Reporting numbers without context or units.

function: Reports a quantitative finding from analysis or experiment
template: The analysis of ___ revealed that ___.
slots: the contrast affects the grating strength
when_to_use: When presenting a key numerical result from modeling or measurement.
common_mistakes: Reporting numbers without units or context.

function: Justifies the choice of a specific method or component for comparison
template: The choice of ___ for creating the light guide was based on ___.
when_to_use: When comparing different technical options in the literature or method selection.

function: Compares advantages and disadvantages of different prior methods
template: Compared to ___, the approach of ___ offers ___ but is limited by ___.
slots: a key limitation (e.g., lower multiplexing capacity)
when_to_use: During the critical analysis of existing literature.
common_mistakes: Providing an unbalanced comparison without acknowledging merits of other methods.

function: Explicitly states the research gap in the literature
template: The literature reveals a gap in the understanding/application of ___ concerning ___.
slots: a neglected aspect or unsolved problem
when_to_use: To transition from the literature review to the proposed work.
common_mistakes: Stating a gap that is already well-addressed in recent work.

function: Identifies a limitation in existing work or methods
template: A key limitation of previous approaches is ___.
when_to_use: In the introduction or survey to motivate the need for the current research.
common_mistakes: Stating limitations without evidence or reference.

function: Classifies existing works or approaches into categories
template: Methods for creating ___ can be classified into ___ based on ___.
when_to_use: When reviewing literature to structure the state-of-the-art.
common_mistakes: Using a classification that is not well-defined or mutually exclusive.

function: Signals the end of a section and leads into a summary
template: The analysis of ___ presented above leads to the following conclusions.
when_to_use: Before a 'Conclusions' sub-section within a chapter.
common_mistakes: Abruptly ending a section without a transition phrase.
